methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Scout Space, founded by Eric Ingram and Sergio Gallucci, is a Series A-stage company in Space & Aerospace that has raised $18M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, though narrow-filter peer median analysis was not viable due to insufficient same-stage datapoints (0 identified) within the immediate peer set.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology was the applicable triangulation approach. Using the $18M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $36M. This method rests on the assumption that Scout Space will experience proportional capital-step-up dynamics consistent with frontier aerospace company trajectory. Public-comp anchoring could not be applied in the absence of disclosed revenue figures, which is standard for private companies at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $31M (low) to $41M (high), with a median point of $36M. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data precluding P/S multiple triangulation, limited same-stage peer comparables for direct median benchmarking, and unknown operational milestones since the $18M raise. Validation of this range would benefit from additional disclosure on run rate or near-term deployment metrics.
